instruction: Fix the bug in `compute_data_size()` function in R/utilities.R where the default `width` is miscalculated when some panels are empty. The issue occurs when using `geom_col()` in a `facet_grid()` where some panels have no data - the plot renders blank instead of showing the data. The bug was introduced by incorrectly using `drop = FALSE` in the `split()` call when calculating resolution across panels. The fix should ensure that empty factor levels are properly handled.
context:
The `compute_data_size()` function calculates data dimensions (width/height) for geoms. When `panels = "across"`, it splits data by PANEL factor and calculates resolution. The relevant code section:

r
if (panels == "across") {
    res <- split(data[[var]], data$PANEL, drop = FALSE)  # BUG: should be drop = TRUE
    res <- vapply(res, resolution, FUN.VALUE = numeric(1), ...)
    res <- min(res, na.rm = TRUE)
}


When a PANEL factor has levels with no corresponding data (e.g., levels c("A", "B") but only "B" has data), using `drop = FALSE` creates an empty vector for the missing level, which causes `resolution()` to return unexpected values.

fail_to_pass: test_that("compute_data_size handles gnarly cases", { df <- data_frame0(x = seq(0, 20, by = 2), PANEL = factor(rep("B", 11), levels = c("A", "B"))); new <- compute_data_size(df, size = NULL, target = "width", default = 1); expect_all_equal(new$width, 2) })
pass_to_pass: Existing tests for compute_data_size with normal panel data

key_changes: Change `drop = FALSE` to `drop = TRUE` in the `split()` call within `compute_data_size()` function
This ensures empty factor levels are dropped before calculating resolution
potential_pitfalls: Using `drop = FALSE` preserves empty factor levels, which causes `resolution()` to return Inf or incorrect values for empty vectors
The fix must maintain backward compatibility with panels that have all levels populated
solution.reference_diff:
diff --git a/DESCRIPTION b/DESCRIPTION
index 01f91fcb9a..da78c46c3d 100644
--- a/DESCRIPTION
+++ b/DESCRIPTION
@@ -70,7 +70,7 @@ Suggests:
     rpart,
     sf (>= 0.7-3),
     svglite (>= 2.1.2),
-    testthat (>= 3.1.5),
+    testthat (>= 3.3.0),
     tibble,
     vdiffr (>= 1.0.6),
     xml2
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index a252538302..714784d06f 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -4,6 +4,8 @@
   (@lgaborini, #6724)
 * Added new argument `geom_curve(shape)` that will be passed down to 
   `grid::curveGrob()` (@fmarotta, #5998).
+* Fixed a regression where default `width` was miscalculated when some panels
+  are empty (@teunbrand, #6758)
 
 # ggplot2 4.0.1
 
diff --git a/R/utilities.R b/R/utilities.R
index 598b9a8fc3..2aa6ab5b42 100644
--- a/R/utilities.R
+++ b/R/utilities.R
@@ -927,7 +927,7 @@ compute_data_size <- function(data, size, default = 0.9,
   panels <- arg_match0(panels, c("across", "by", "ignore"))
 
   if (panels == "across") {
-    res <- split(data[[var]], data$PANEL, drop = FALSE)
+    res <- split(data[[var]], data$PANEL, drop = TRUE)
     res <- vapply(res, resolution, FUN.VALUE = numeric(1), ...)
     res <- min(res, na.rm = TRUE)
   } else if (panels == "by") {
